title: galium subvelutinum subsp. kurdicum
intro: Galium subvelutinum subsp. kurdicum, also known as Kurdish bedstraw, is a subspecies of the Galium genus, a type of flowering plant. Kurdish bedstraw has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, making it useful in treating wounds, cuts, and skin irritations. The plant is believed to have various health benefits, such as reducing fever, relieving pain, and promoting digestive health. Kurdish bedstraw can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, or tinctures for its medicinal uses, although more research is needed to confirm its efficacy and safety.
